title: Review and setlist: Leslie Odom Jr. brings Broadway to the Boston Pops
author: Shannon Murphy
bullets: Leslie Odom Jr. performed with the Boston Pops at Symphony Hall on May 22, 2026, as part of their spring season.
The concert featured American classics and Odom Jr.'s renditions of 'Fly Me To The Moon' and 'Wait for It' from Hamilton.
Odom Jr.'s performance included the song 'A Change is Gonna Come,' highlighting his versatility and connection to American music history.

title: Chelsea man facing slew of assault charges for alleged violence against young children
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Kener Bautista-Zepeda, 19, from Chelsea, faces 12 charges related to assaults on two children under 14.
Incidents include burning a child with a cigarette lighter and another with a heated fork, according to prosecutors.
Bautista-Zepeda, arrested in Virginia, pleaded not guilty; a dangerousness hearing is set for May 28.

title: Concord man, 73, convicted of raping 20-year-old in Sandwich restaurant bathroom in 2023
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Elie Jammal, 73, of Concord, was convicted of raping a 20-year-old in a Sandwich restaurant bathroom in July 2023.
Jammal was sentenced to 13.5 to 15 years in prison for rape, followed by five years of probation for assault.
The court highlighted Jammal's actions as motivated by entitlement; victim was supported by family and police.

title: Newton Church, founded in 1893, is becoming condos
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Newton's Church of the Open Word, built in 1893, is being converted into six condos on 19 Highland Ave.
Developer Habitech Communities purchased the property for $4 million to redevelop it, adding a new five-story building with 26 condos.
The conversion reflects a Greater Boston trend as former churches become residences amidst shrinking congregations and rising housing needs.

title: 5 players the Celtics could target this offseason outside of Giannis Antetokounmpo
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: The Celtics may target Kawhi Leonard if he's available for trade, offering defensive and scoring versatility.
LeBron James, an unrestricted free agent, could join the Celtics, providing veteran leadership and facilitating skills.
John Collins, a potential forward option, averaged 13 points and five rebounds, with a shooting accuracy above 55% last season.

title: 2 local college baseball programs advance to NCAA World Series
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Bentley University advanced to the NCAA Division II World Series for the first time after defeating Molloy University in Waltham.
Endicott College secured its fourth consecutive Division III championship round appearance by beating Salve Regina University in Newport.
Bentley will compete in North Carolina and Endicott in Ohio; championship opponents and schedules are yet to be announced.

title: Celtics reportedly plan to work out UConn basketball star ahead of NBA Draft
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Boston Celtics plan to work out UConn forward Alex Karaban ahead of the 2026 NBA Draft.
Karaban, ranked No. 32 on ESPN's top 100 prospects, played four years at UConn, winning two NCAA titles.
The Celtics hold the 27th and 40th picks in the draft; Karaban averaged 12.5 points and 5.0 rebounds at UConn.

title: Review & setlist: Charlie Puth turns MGM Music Hall into a masterclass in musicianship
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Charlie Puth performed at Boston's MGM Music Hall on May 22, 2026, as part of his 'Whatever's Clever! World Tour'.
The Berklee alumnus mixed jazz and pop, showcasing musicianship through innovative renditions of both new and older songs.
Puth's concert emphasized vocal skill and music theory, avoiding spectacle for a focused, sophisticated musical experience.

title: Review and setlist: Jakob Dylan’s choice to channel Tom Petty pays off at Beverly Wallflowers show
author: Peter Chianca
bullets: The Wallflowers performed 'Bringing Down the Horse' in full at The Cabot in Beverly on May 22, 2026.
Jakob Dylan's band followed their album with Tom Petty's 'Long After Dark,' marking a unique tour setlist choice.
Encore included 'I've Been Delivered,' 'Everything I Need,' and covers of Petty's 'Refugee' and 'The Waiting.'

title: Here’s where people are moving — and leaving — in Mass., according to new U.S. Census data
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Boston lost 1,338 residents last year, continuing a decline of 5,644 people since 2020, amid high housing costs.
Everett and Worcester saw the largest population gains in Massachusetts for 2025, adding 2,362 and 1,916 residents respectively.
Massachusetts' population grew by 15,500 people in 2025, a 0.2% increase, largely attributed to immigration.
